title: Sargentodoxa cuneata
intro: Sargentodoxa cuneata, commonly known as Wheeler's Bloodroot or He Shou Wu, is a perennial vine native to China. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-aging, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to improve cardiovascular health, boost immune function, and reduce the risk of certain diseases. The bioactive constituents of Sargentodoxa cuneata include polypeptides, polysaccharides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Sargentodoxa cuneata is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and is also used to make capsules, tablets, and other herbal supplements.
taxonomy: Sargentodoxa cuneata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Menispermaceae. It belongs to the genus Sargentodoxa. The specific epithet 'cuneata' refers to the shape of its leaves. This species is native to East Asia and is also known as the Wheeler's fang palm or Wheeler's fang fern, though it is not a true palm or fern. The plant is also known as Wheeler's fang, Wheeler's fang vine, or Wheeler's fang.
